message:
fix(providers): handle cannot_be_a_base URLs gracefully in all provider URL normalizers

url::Url::parse() can succeed on inputs like "data:text/plain,hello" or
other opaque-scheme URIs where path manipulation (append, replace, strip)
is meaningless or panics. All five normalize functions (anthropic, openai,
openai_responses, codex_responses, cohere) now check url.cannot_be_a_base()
and fall through to the string-based fallback path when the parsed URL
lacks a proper authority/path structure.

The fallback path already handles all the suffix-matching logic correctly,
so the fix is purely defensive: extract the string representation from
the parsed URL, trim trailing slashes, and let the existing string logic
produce the final endpoint URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
